name: Dashboard renders a layout-shaped skeleton during the SSR → island-fetch gap
description: Every authenticated page now uses a top-level data island that fetches on mount. The first render — both server-side and pre-hydration on the client — shows a shimmer skeleton matching the page layout (hero placeholder + 4 KPI tiles + 2 grid panels for /dashboard). No literal 'Loading dashboard…' string ever surfaces.

persona.name: Any logged-in user on a cold tab
persona.context: Hasn't been on /dashboard yet this session — no in-memory fetch cache.

preconditions: Browser cache cleared OR fresh tab

steps.expect: Within the first paint: shimmer skeleton renders (hero block + 4 KPI cards + 2 panel rows). Visible CSS animation 'pmShimmer' on each block (1.4s ease-in-out).

steps.action: Island mounts, useEffect fires fetches in parallel: /analytics/dashboard, /jobs, /quotes, /invoices, /customers, /notifications
steps.expect: All resolve in <800ms on warm path; skeleton is replaced by the live data render — no 'Loading dashboard…' literal ever flashes
Skeleton renders; after timeout, error pill 'Couldn't load dashboard: …' replaces it (graceful degradation)

evaluationCriteria.check: Skeleton blocks match the height of the real content they're replacing (hero ~200px, KPI tile ~110px, panel rows ~280px) so there's no layout jump on hydration
Visual shimmer reads as 'app is working' rather than 'app is broken'; cold-path → live data feels like a smooth fade
ShimmerStyle keyframes are inlined inside the skeleton component so even SSR HTML carries the animation; same skeleton primitives are shared across all 7 page islands
Skeleton elements are aria-hidden so screen readers don't read out a series of meaningless 'group' / 'image' announcements
